description: A simulation produces different results on repeated runs with the same seed because parallel reductions summed in varying order.
body: Failure mode: a parallel reduction accumulates floating-point contributions in a nondeterministic order, so rounding differences make the same-seed run produce different bits each time. Detection signals: results that differ across runs despite a fixed seed, divergence that grows with thread count, and reproducibility returning when run single-threaded. Do not treat the variation as acceptable noise in a regression. Safer fallback: enforce a deterministic reduction order or use a reproducible reduction, then confirm same-seed runs match across thread counts.
